\:return: declaration containing the variable

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_variable_by_name
documentation:
Get a variable or parameter node based on the name
\:param node: the neuron or synapse containing the parameter
\:param var_name: variable name to be searched
\:return: the node if found, otherwise None

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_state_variable_by_name
documentation:
Get a state variable node based on the name
\:param node: the neuron or synapse containing the parameter
\:param var_name: variable name to be searched
\:return: the node if found, otherwise None

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_state_variable_declaration_by_name
documentation:
Get the declaration based on the name of the parameter
\:param node: the neuron or synapse containing the parameter
\:param var_name: variable name to be searched
\:return: declaration containing the variable if found, otherwise None

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.replace_post_moved_variable_names
documentation: In the synapse, continuous-valued input ports could be referred to based on their name. When they are moved to the neuron, they need to be referred to by the variable name as it exists on the neuron side. This function performs the variable name replacement recursively in `astnode`. `astnode` can also be a list of nodes.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.collect_variable_names_in_expression
documentation:
Collect all occurrences of variables (`ASTVariable`) XXX ...
\:param expr: expression to collect the variables from
\:return: a list of variables

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_declarations_from_block
documentation:
Get declarations from the given block containing the given variable on the left-hand side.

```
param var_name

variable name

param block

block to collect the variable declarations

return

a list of declarations
```

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.recursive_dependent_variables_search
documentation:
Collect all the variable names used in the defining expressions of a list of variables.
\:param vars: list of variable names moved from synapse to neuron
\:param model: ASTModel to perform the recursive search
\:return: list of variable names from the recursive search

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.recursive_necessary_variables_search
documentation:
Collect all the variable names used in the defining expressions of a list of variables.
\:param vars: list of variable names moved from synapse to neuron
\:param model: ASTModel to perform the recursive search
\:return: list of variable names from the recursive search

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.remove_initial_values_for_kernels
documentation: Remove initial values for original declarations (e.g. g_in, g_in', V_m); these might conflict with the initial value expressions returned from ODE-toolbox.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.update_initial_values_for_odes
documentation: Update initial values for original ODE declarations (e.g. V_m', g_ahp'') that are present in the model before ODE-toolbox processing, with the formatted variable names and initial values returned by ODE-toolbox.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_necessary_variables
documentation: Return a list of all right-hand side variables in the model that a certain, given left-hand side variable `var` depends on.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_dependent_variables
documentation: Return a list of all left-hand side variables in the model that depend on `var` in their right-hand side.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_all_variables_names_in_expression
documentation: Get variable names of any order (foo, foo', foo'', etc. will result in "foo" being added to the list returned)

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.create_integrate_odes_combinations
documentation: Visit all integrate_odes() calls in the model, compose these as a list of strings, and set them as a model private member (`model.integrate_odes_combinations`).

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_all_integrate_odes_calls_unique
documentation: Get a list of all unique `integrate_odes()` function calls in the model (i.e. each having a different set of parameters).

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.create_initial_values_for_kernels
documentation: Add the variables used in kernels from the ode-toolbox result dictionary as ODEs in NESTML AST

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.transform_ode_and_kernels_to_json
documentation:
Converts AST node to a JSON representation suitable for passing to ode-toolbox.

Each kernel has to be generated for each spike buffer convolve in which it occurs, e.g. if the NESTML model code contains the statements

> ```
> convolve(G, exc_spikes)
> convolve(G, inh_spikes)
> ```

then `kernel_buffers` will contain the pairs `(G, exc_spikes)` and `(G, inh_spikes)`, from which two ODEs will be generated, with dynamical state (variable) names `G__X__exc_spikes` and `G__X__inh_spikes`.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.remove_ode_definitions_from_equations_block
documentation: Removes all ODE definitions from all equations blocks in the model.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_delta_factors_
documentation: For every occurrence of a convolution of the form `x^(n) = a * convolve(kernel, inport) + ...` where `kernel` is a delta function, add the element `(x^(n), inport) --> a` to the set.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.remove_kernel_definitions_from_equations_block
documentation: Removes all kernels in equations blocks.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.add_timestep_symbol
documentation: Add timestep variable to the internals block

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.generate_kernel_buffers
documentation: For every occurrence of a convolution of the form `convolve(var, spike_buf)`: add the element `(kernel, spike_buf)` to the set, with `kernel` being the kernel that contains variable `var`.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.replace_convolution_aliasing_inlines
documentation: Replace all occurrences of kernel names (e.g. `I_dend` and `I_dend'` for a definition involving a second-order kernel `inline kernel I_dend = convolve(kern_name, spike_buf)`) with the ODE-toolbox generated variable `kern_name__X__spike_buf`.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.replace_variable_names_in_expressions
documentation:
Replace all occurrences of variables names in NESTML format (e.g. `g_ex$''`)` with the ode-toolbox formatted
variable name (e.g. `g_ex__DOLLAR__d__d`).

Variables aliasing convolutions should already have been covered by replace_convolution_aliasing_inlines().

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.replace_convolve_calls_with_buffers_
documentation: Replace all occurrences of `convolve(kernel[']^n, spike_input_port)` with the corresponding buffer variable, e.g. `g_E__X__spikes_exc[__d]^n` for a kernel named `g_E` and a spike input port named `spikes_exc`.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.update_blocktype_for_common_parameters
documentation: Change the BlockType for all homogeneous parameters to BlockType.COMMON_PARAMETER

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_convolve_function_calls
documentation:
Returns all sum function calls in the handed over meta_model node or one of its children.
\:param nodes: a single or list of AST nodes.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.contains_convolve_function_call
documentation:
Indicates whether \_ast or one of its child nodes contains a sum call.
\:param ast: a single meta_model
\:return: True if sum is contained, otherwise False.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_function_calls
documentation:
For a handed over list of function names, this method retrieves all function calls in the meta_model.
\:param ast_node: a single meta_model node
\:param function_list: a list of function names
\:return: a list of all functions in the meta_model

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.resolve_to_variable_symbol_in_blocks
documentation: Resolve a variable (by name) to its corresponding `Symbol` within the AST blocks in `blocks`.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils._find_port_in_dict
documentation:
Finds the corresponding "inhibitory" port for a given "excitatory" port and vice versa in the handed over map.
\:param rport_to_port_map: map containing NESTML port names for the rport
\:param port: port to be searched
\:return: key value in the map if the port is found, else None

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_spike_input_ports_in_pairs
documentation:
Returns a list of spike input ports in pairs in case of input port qualifiers.
The result of this function is used to construct a vector that provides a mapping to the NESTML spike buffer index. The vector looks like below:
.. code-block:

```
[ {AMPA_SPIKES, GABA_SPIKES}, {NMDA_SPIKES, -1} ]
```

where the vector index is the NEST rport number. The value is a tuple containing the NESTML index(es) to the spike buffer.
In case if the rport is shared between two NESTML buffers, the vector element contains the tuple of the form (excitatory_port_index, inhibitory_port_index). Otherwise, the tuple is of the form (spike_port_index, -1).

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.assign_numeric_non_numeric_state_variables
documentation:
For each ASTVariable, set the `node._is_numeric` member to True or False based on whether this variable will be solved with the analytic or numeric solver.

Ideally, this would not be a property of the ASTVariable as it is an implementation detail (that only emerges during code generation) and not an intrinsic part of the model itself. However, this approach is preferred over setting it as a property of the variable printers as it would have to make each printer aware of all models and variables therein.

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.depends_only_on_vars
documentation: Returns True if and only if all variables that occur in `expr` are in `vars`

methods.name: pynestml.utils.ast_utils.ASTUtils.get_on_receive_blocks_by_input_port_name
documentation: Get the onReceive blocks in the model associated with a given input port.
A collection of helpful methods for AST manipulation.
